meaning: A bank account where you deposit money to save it, which usually earns interest over time.
example: It is smart to transfer a portion of your monthly salary to a savings account.

meaning: A bank account used for everyday transactions, from which money can be easily withdrawn or spent.
example: I use my checking account to pay my monthly rent and buy groceries.

meaning: The total amount of money in a bank account at a specific time.
example: Please check your account balance to make sure you have enough money for the transfer.

meaning: The percentage of a sum of money charged by a lender or paid by a bank to a saver.
example: The bank raised its interest rate on savings accounts to four percent.

meaning: To put money into a bank account.
example: I need to go to the bank to deposit this check into my account.

meaning: The act of taking money out of a bank account.
example: She made a cash withdrawal of one hundred dollars from the ATM.

meaning: A payment method where money is sent electronically straight into a recipient's bank account.
example: My salary is paid via direct deposit, so it is in my account every Friday morning.

meaning: A printed or digital record showing all the deposits, withdrawals, and fees in a bank account.
example: I review my monthly bank statement to track my spending habits.

meaning: An arrangement or situation where you withdraw more money from your account than you actually have.
example: Her account went into overdraft after the rent check was processed.

meaning: A charge imposed by a bank when a customer withdraws more money than is available in their account.
example: I had to pay a thirty-dollar overdraft fee because my balance fell below zero.

meaning: A bank account shared by two or more people, typically family members or business partners.
example: My husband and I opened a joint account to manage our household expenses together.

meaning: A local office of a bank or other large organization.
example: I need to visit the local branch to speak with a loan officer.

meaning: A bank employee who deals directly with customers, handling deposits and withdrawals.
example: The bank teller greeted me warmly and helped me deposit my money.

meaning: A machine that dispenses cash and performs other banking services when you insert a bank card.
example: I need to find an ATM to withdraw some cash before going to the market.

meaning: A secret number that you use with a bank card to withdraw money or make payments.
example: Never share your PIN with anyone, not even bank employees.

meaning: A locked box inside a bank vault used for storing valuable items like jewelry or important papers.
example: We keep our property deeds and old family gold in a safe deposit box.

meaning: A nine-digit code that identifies a specific financial institution in banking transactions.
example: You will need both your bank routing number and account number to set up direct deposit.

meaning: A unique set of numbers used to identify a specific bank account.
example: Please double-check your account number before confirming the transfer.

meaning: A service that allows customers to conduct financial transactions using a smartphone app.
example: Mobile banking makes it easy to deposit checks using your phone camera.

meaning: Conducting banking transactions over the internet through a bank's website.
example: I logged into online banking to pay my utility bills last night.

meaning: A savings product that earns a fixed interest rate in exchange for keeping your money untouched for a set period.
example: He put his savings into a one-year certificate of deposit to get a higher interest rate.

meaning: The lowest amount of money that a customer must keep in a bank account to avoid fees.
example: This checking account requires a minimum balance of five hundred dollars.

meaning: A situation where many customers rush to withdraw their money from a bank because they fear it will fail.
example: The government stepped in to prevent a bank run after rumors spread about the bank's stability.

meaning: A professional who helps individuals manage their money, investments, and financial planning.
example: We met with a financial advisor to plan for our children's college education.

meaning: A number representing a person's creditworthiness, based on their history of paying back debts.
example: Paying your credit card bills on time is the best way to improve your credit score.

meaning: A cooperative financial institution owned and controlled by its members, often offering lower fees.
example: She decided to join a local credit union instead of a major national bank.

meaning: A person designated to receive the funds or assets from an account in the event of the owner's death.
example: He listed his daughter as the sole beneficiary of his savings account.

meaning: The ease with which assets or savings can be quickly converted into cash without losing value.
example: Cash in a checking account has high liquidity, while real estate has low liquidity.

meaning: Interest calculated on both the initial principal and the accumulated interest from previous periods.
example: Thanks to compound interest, even small savings can grow significantly over thirty years.

meaning: An asset that a borrower offers to a lender as security for a loan.
example: She had to put up her house as collateral to secure the business loan.

meaning: To fail to pay a debt or loan by the required time.
example: If you default on your car loan, the bank may take the car back.

meaning: The original sum of money lent or invested, not including the interest.
example: Her monthly mortgage payments go toward paying off both the principal and the interest.

meaning: The process of gradually paying off a debt over time through regular, equal payments.
example: The bank provided an amortization schedule showing how the loan would be paid off over fifteen years.

meaning: The maximum amount of money a credit card company allows you to borrow or charge on your card.
example: He reached his credit limit and could not make any more purchases until he paid off his balance.

meaning: The yearly interest rate charged for borrowing money or earned through an investment.
example: Compare the APR of different credit cards to find the one with the lowest borrowing costs.

meaning: A specific type of loan used to purchase a house or real estate, where the property is security for the loan.
example: They took out a thirty-year mortgage to buy their first home.

meaning: An amount of money that is owed to a person, bank, or company.
example: He worked hard to pay off all his college debt within five years.

meaning: A legal status of a person or organization that cannot repay the debts they owe to creditors.
example: The retail chain declared bankruptcy after several years of falling sales.

meaning: A person who promises to pay a debt if the borrower fails to do so.
example: Since the student had no income, her father signed as a guarantor for the apartment lease.
